city_agency: ICE via local/state police (Danville IL records)
headline: ICE Taps into Nationwide AI-Enabled Camera Network Data Shows
summary: Records from Danville IL revealed 4,000+ Flock lookups nationwide tagged ICE/ICE+ERO/immigration; ICE has no contract but got de facto access via local cops searching on its behalf.

city_agency: Johnson County TX Sheriff (reached WA/IL cameras)
headline: A Texas Cop Searched License Plate Cameras Nationwide for a Woman Who Got an Abortion
summary: Officer ran 83,000+ camera search logged 'had an abortion, search for female'; sheriff/Flock called it a welfare check but court records later showed a death investigation.

headline: Police Said They Surveilled Woman Who Had an Abortion for Her Safety. Court Records Show They Considered Charging Her
summary: Court records showed the search was a 'death investigation of a non-viable fetus' and police consulted the DA about charging the woman, contradicting the public welfare-check narrative.

headline: CBP Had Access to More than 80000 Flock AI Cameras Nationwide
summary: CBP accessed 80,000+ Flock cameras via a largely undisclosed pilot; many departments were unaware they shared with federal immigration authorities. Flock paused federal pilots.

city_agency: ICE/HSI Secret Service Navy (via Sen. Wyden)
headline: ICE Secret Service Navy All Had Access to Flocks Nationwide Network of Cameras
summary: A Wyden letter revealed ICE HSI, Secret Service, and Navy criminal investigators accessed Flock's network without public oversight.

headline: How Cops Are Using Flock Safetys ALPR Network to Surveil Protesters and Activists
summary: Analysis of 12M+ searches by 3,900+ agencies found 50+ agencies ran protest-related searches (No Kings, Hands Off, 50501).

headline: License Plate Surveillance Logs Reveal Racist Policing Against Romani People
summary: 80+ agencies ran Flock searches using anti-Romani slurs/stereotypes, often with no crime listed.

headline: Congress Launches Investigation into Flock After 404 Media Reporting
summary: House Oversight ranking members opened a formal investigation into Flock over abortion/immigration tracking and demanded all ICE/CBP/abortion search logs.

headline: Flock Removes States From National Lookup Tool After ICE and Abortion Searches Revealed
summary: Flock blocked national-lookup access to CA, IL, and VA cameras after the ICE and abortion revelations and new state laws.

headline: EFFs Investigations Expose Flock Safetys Surveillance Abuses 2025 in Review
summary: Master synthesis of 12M+ searches by 3,900+ agencies documenting protest surveillance, anti-Romani searches, and reproductive-care tracking.

headline: Flock Exposed Its AI-Powered Cameras to the Internet. We Tracked Ourselves
summary: ~60 Flock Condor PTZ cameras were left on the open internet with no authentication, exposing live feeds and 30 days of archived video including children.

headline: Lawmakers say stolen police logins are exposing Flock surveillance cameras to hackers
summary: Lawmakers asked the FTC to investigate Flock after 35+ customer passwords were found stolen via infostealer malware and sold on a Russian cybercrime forum.

headline: AI Surveillance Unmasking Flock Safetys Insecurities
summary: Researcher Jon Gaines documented 51 vulnerabilities (22 CVEs) incl. an unauthenticated root shell (CVE-2025-59403) on Flock devices running end-of-life Android.

headline: Researcher Turns Insecure License Plate Cameras Into Open Source Surveillance Tool
summary: A researcher found Motorola ReaperHD ALPR cameras streaming plate data unauthenticated to the open internet; a tool scraped plates into a spreadsheet.

headline: Glendale police used an ethnic slur to search a license plate surveillance database
summary: A Glendale investigator used an anti-Romani slur as a Flock search term in July 2024; EFF found hundreds of similar slur-based searches nationally.

headline: Your car is being tracked by Flock cameras and heres who can access that data in Arizona
summary: Mesa officers searched Flock data for DHS requests marked 'immigration violations'; ~40,000 external searches of Chandler data in one month prompted a contract pause.

headline: Sedona council pauses camera program owned and operated by Flock Safety
summary: Sedona paused then ended its 11-camera program after Flock acknowledged CBP had accessed local data despite assurances the city would control access.

headline: Border Patrol is monitoring US drivers and detaining those with suspicious travel patterns
summary: AP revealed a secretive Border Patrol ALPR program flagging drivers by travel pattern, identifying Phoenix-area cameras and ~1,600 Flock readers across 22 states.

headline: California Auditor Releases Damning Report About Law Enforcements Use of ALPRs
summary: State Auditor report 2019-118 found none of four agencies met SB 34 safeguards; LAPD held ~320M images with no policy and agencies shared data across 44 states.

headline: Ex-Costa Mesa police officer pleads guilty to using FLOCK cameras to track his mistress
summary: Officer Robert Josett pleaded guilty after using Flock and CLETS to track his mistress, her boyfriend, his ex, and wife; 3 years probation + DV program.

headline: Ex-RSO deputy who stalked former fiancee harassed volunteer sentenced
summary: Deputy Alexander Vanny used Flock to track his ex's friend; convicted of stalking and unauthorized data use among other counts; 6 years prison.

headline: SFPD let outside cops search city surveillance data for ICE
summary: SFPD allowed out-of-state agencies ~1.6M searches of its Flock data incl. 19+ ICE-related, violating SB 34/SB 54.

headline: San Francisco Police Audit Shows Feds Improperly Accessed License Plate Data Hundreds of Times
summary: A follow-up SFPD audit found 299 illegal federal/out-of-state queries via NCRIC and Western States Information Network analysts; access disabled.

city_agency: City of El Cajon / El Cajon PD
headline: California sues El Cajon over police use of license plate data
summary: AG Bonta sued El Cajon for sharing Flock data with 100+ out-of-state agencies in violation of SB 34 after it refused to stop.

headline: Oakland police hit with lawsuit for sharing license plate camera data with feds
summary: Secure Justice sued Oakland alleging OPD shared ALPR data with federal authorities incl. direct access via the Crime Tracer tool.

headline: California Activists Sue Marin County Sheriff for Illegally Sharing Drivers License Plate Data With ICE CBP
summary: First lawsuit challenging ALPR sharing; settlement barred federal/out-of-state agencies from querying Marin County data.

headline: California Department of Justice Declares Out-of-State Sharing of License Plate Data Unlawful
summary: AG Bonta issued bulletins declaring out-of-state/federal ALPR sharing a SB 34 violation after ~71 CA agencies were found sharing data out of state.

headline: Chula Vista Police Chief Says She Didnt Know Department Shared Data With Feds
summary: ICE and CBP appeared on Chula Vista PD's ALPR share report, a potential SB 34/SB 54 violation; sharing stopped after public outcry.

headline: San Diego County police agencies access many private license plate readers with minimal oversight
summary: KPBS found SD County agencies tap private ALPR networks with little oversight and some gave side-door access to ICE.

headline: Newsom just vetoed a bill to regulate license plate readers even as fresh evidence of misuse emerges
summary: Newsom vetoed SB 274 amid evidence of 100+ out-of-state-sharing violations and Riverside deputies adding 700+ plates to hotlists with vague justifications.

headline: Lawsuit SMUD and Sacramento Police Violate State Law by Sharing Data Without a Warrant
summary: EFF alleged the utility gave police zip-code energy data used for drive-bys and plate-running that disproportionately targeted Asian American residents.

headline: City Council terminates Mountain Views license plate camera contract
summary: Audit found out-of-state/federal access for ~3 months and ~600,000 searches by 250+ unapproved agencies; council terminated and shut off all 30 cameras.

headline: Oxnard Police Department Suspends Use of Flock Safety ALPRs
summary: A vendor 'nationwide query' feature let outside/federal agencies query OPD data despite a California-only setting; OPD suspended its cameras.

headline: Flock license plate readers shared data with out-of-state agencies Ventura County audit finds
summary: National lookup, disabled in 2023, was somehow reactivated allowing ~364,000 out-of-state queries in a month; 299 referenced immigration.

headline: San Jose Residents Sue City Saying Flock Safety Cameras Allow Mass Surveillance
summary: Institute for Justice federal class action argues the 474-camera network is warrantless suspicionless surveillance; 1,000+ employees can search.

headline: California drivers accuse Flock Safety of sharing data with federal and out-of-state agencies
summary: Class action alleges Flock enabled federal/out-of-state access in violation of the CA ALPR Privacy Act, citing a CHP search of 845 CA databases for ICE.

headline: After police used Flock cameras to accuse a Denver woman of theft she had to prove her own innocence
summary: Sgt. Jamie Milliman tracked a woman via Flock and summoned her for a $25 package theft; she proved innocence with her own footage. Officer disciplined.

headline: Frequent immigration-related searches of Denver Flock camera data revealed in logs
summary: Denver Flock data was searched 1,400+ times for ICE/immigration since June 2024 despite policy; Denver disabled national lookup and later ended its contract.

headline: Police audit finds no evidence of misuse by Thornton officer who ran more than 10000 Flock searches
summary: A complaint alleged ~20,000 searches by one officer; an audit found 10,318 searches consistent with stolen-vehicle-list policy and cleared the officer.

headline: 2 Boulder residents sue over citys use of Flock cameras citing widespread privacy violations
summary: Residents filed a proposed class action alleging the 31-camera network violates the Colorado Constitution; network was on national sharing 3+ years.

headline: Where are police Flock cameras CT I-Team dives into search data
summary: Out-of-state agencies (mostly Houston PD) ran thousands of immigration/ICE searches against CT departments' data; drove state law SB 4 restricting sharing.

headline: Lawsuit License Plate Readers Found in CT Violate 4th Amendment
summary: Institute for Justice challenged Flock's warrantless 30-day vehicle tracking, documenting unrestricted access across 21 CT departments.

headline: How Cops Are Using Flock Safetys ALPR Network to Surveil Protesters and Activists
summary: Delaware State Police ran nine queries in March 2025 targeting Direct Action Everywhere animal-rights activists, tied to a Mountaire Farms investigation.

headline: Orange City officer accused of stalking woman using license plate readers
summary: Officer Jarmarus Brown ran his girlfriend's plate 69 times and family members' plates dozens more, plus planted an AirTag; charged and notified of termination.

headline: Keys deputy misused official databases to pursue woman he met on set of Bad Monkey
summary: Deputy Lamar Roman added a woman's car to an ALPR hotlist to track her then pulled her over; fired and arrested. (Used DAVID/NCIC plus ALPR.)

headline: Niceville officer arrested after internal database investigation
summary: Officer Coty Hall used Flock to track another officer and harass that officer's spouse; fired and pleaded no contest.

city_agency: Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission
headline: Floridas Wildlife Cops Are Searching Thousands of Flock Cameras for ICE
summary: FWC officers deputized under 287(g) ran Flock searches for ICE across thousands of camera networks despite Flock's claim it does not work with ICE.

headline: Braselton chief arrested accused of using police cameras to stalk people
summary: GBI arrested Chief Michael Steffman for using ALPR to stalk citizens; charged with violation of oath, stalking, and misuse of ALPR systems; later found dead.

headline: Ex-Georgia sheriffs deputy charged with stalking illegally using license plate data
summary: Deputy Chris Rozar was arrested after a GBI investigation for using the sheriff's Flock system to surveil a woman he was interested in; fired.

headline: Sandy Springs used Flock cameras to aid ICE immigration enforcement data shows
summary: Records showed Sandy Springs PD ran national-network searches on behalf of ICE incl. searches logged 'ero'.

headline: Atlanta PD used Flock cameras to track migrants records show
summary: Two APD accounts ran 15 searches in March 2025 using terms 'locate alien' and 'ERO assist'.

headline: City Learns Flock Accessed Cameras in Childrens Gymnastics Room as a Sales Pitch Demo Renews Contract Anyway
summary: Flock employees accessed JCC cameras incl. a children's gymnastics room during sales demos; Dunwoody renewed its contract anyway.

headline: Jerome County sheriff used Flock cameras to search for wifes vehicle documents show
summary: Sheriff George Oppedyk queried his wife's plate hundreds of times; retired mid-investigation. Idaho AG declined to charge citing insufficient evidence.

headline: Tort claim accuses Caldwells Flock Safety camera program of violating Idaho privacy law
summary: A resident filed a tort claim arguing Caldwell's contract lets Flock use plate data beyond Idaho law permits.

headline: Mount Prospect police probed for sharing Illinois license plate reader data in Texas abortion case
summary: The Texas abortion search reached Mount Prospect's cameras; the SoS found 262 immigration-related searches touching its data, violating 2023 Illinois law.

city_agency: Illinois Secretary of State / Flock / CBP
headline: Giannoulias Audit Finds License Plate Reader Company in Violation of State Law
summary: State audit found Flock let CBP access Illinois cameras via an undisclosed pilot and 46 out-of-state agencies ran illegal searches; CBP access ordered off.

headline: State audit finds west suburb shared license plate data with federal immigration agency
summary: Forest Park PD shared Motorola ALPR data with CBP from April 2024 in violation of the Illinois TRUST Act; access disabled at the state's direction.

headline: Feds Used Local Cops Password to Do Immigration Surveillance With Flock Cameras
summary: A DEA agent used a Palos Heights detective's Flock credentials to run 24 'immigration violation' searches, illegal in Illinois and against Flock's terms.

headline: Several states accessed Danville license-plate data for immigration reasons Illinois cracking down
summary: Danville PD records revealed 4,000+ Flock lookups nationally labeled immigration/ICE; helped trigger the statewide crackdown.

headline: Evanston shuts down license plate cameras terminates contract with Flock Safety
summary: Evanston deactivated 19 devices and terminated after the state audit; Flock later reinstalled cameras without permission and had to be ordered to remove them.

headline: Oak Park terminates Flock license plate reader contract
summary: Oak Park canceled its contract after Palos Heights ran 28 'immigration violation' searches that included Oak Park data.

headline: Reported ICE accessing Flock ALPR cameras via local law enforcement (Indiana)
summary: Audit records showed Grant County deputies ran ~238 immigration-related searches described as a 'confidential investigation'; Lake County also ran such searches.

headline: Bloomington Police bans using Flock data for immigration reproductive investigations
summary: Amid ~400 protesters, Bloomington PD updated its policy to prohibit immigration- and reproductive-healthcare-related Flock searches.

headline: License Plate Surveillance Logs Reveal Racist Policing Against Romani People
summary: Sioux City PD searched the phrase 'Traveling Romanian Theft Group' as part of EFF's finding that 80+ agencies used anti-Romani search terms.

headline: Civil rights advocates call for cities to restrict automated license plate readers
summary: A 63-page ACLU/University of Iowa report surveyed ~43 agencies, found Flock dominant and largely unregulated, and documented ICE/abortion-related search risks.

headline: Former Kechi police lieutenant convicted on stalking charges
summary: Lt. Victor Heiar used Wichita's Flock to track his estranged wife; convicted of computer crime and stalking; Wichita revoked Kechi's access.

headline: Sedgwick police chief tracked ex-girlfriend 164 times using license plate cams
summary: Chief Lee Nygaard ran his ex's plate 164 times and her boyfriend's 64 times under false pretexts; resigned and lost certification.

headline: Former Bonner Springs detective used license plate cameras to stalk wife
summary: Det. Kyle Rector allegedly used plate readers to track his estranged wife and two men, plus a hidden bedroom camera; arrested and charged.

headline: A DEA agent used Louisvilles Flock camera database for immigration searches against LMPD policy
summary: A DEA agent used a detective's shared Flock login for immigration searches violating a Louisville ordinance; three officers disciplined.

headline: Former LMPD officer charged with official misconduct stalking harassment
summary: Officer Roberto Cedeno allegedly used ALPR to track an ex-partner and her friends hundreds of times; indicted on multiple felonies.

headline: In Louisville half the people charged last year in license plate reader cases were Black
summary: A KyCIR review found LMPD used Flock readers in 177 citations in a year, half issued to Black people in a city ~25% Black.

headline: How the U.S. Park Police are helping ICE carry out deportation efforts
summary: Court records document Park Police parkway stops turning into warrantless ICE arrests, alongside new Flock cameras on I-295.

headline: License plate reader technology for Baltimore police approved despite public opposition
summary: The Board of Estimates approved a $1.46M ALPR expansion giving BPD a decade of vehicle-location history despite unanimous public opposition.

headline: Flock gives law enforcement all over the country access to your location
summary: 80+ MA departments feed a national network reachable by ~7,000 agencies; Auburn records showed direct ICE access via the Vigilant national database.

headline: Cambridge ends Flock contract
summary: After suspending cameras over ICE/abortion fears, Flock technicians installed two new cameras without the city's knowledge; Cambridge terminated for 'material breach of trust'.

headline: How Cops Are Using Flock Safetys ALPR Network to Surveil Protesters and Activists
summary: Grand Rapids PD ran five Flock searches corresponding to a February rally, hitting ~650 networks with 'Protest' logged as the reason.

headline: Some Twin Cities suburbs shut off license plate cameras over data sharing concerns
summary: Suburbs ended programs after finding outside agencies weren't following Minnesota's case-number and reasonable-suspicion requirements amid an immigration surge.

headline: ACLU of Minnesota finds out-of-state and ICE searches against Minnesota ALPR data
summary: ACLU-MN found access so broad that cameras were searched ~300,000 times per month, incl. prohibited civil-immigration uses; fueled HF 4205.

headline: Joplin officer no longer employed after alleged misuse of license plate tracking system
summary: One officer accounted for ~25% of the dept's Flock usage, searching a single plate ~395 times with vague reasons; left employment, state audit requested.

headline: License Plate Readers In Nebraska Help ICE Conduct Immigration Enforcement Efforts
summary: Audit logs show Douglas County logged 20+ immigration searches and Lancaster County searches tied to a DHS agent, feeding the Glenn Valley Foods raid.

headline: Vegas police are big users of license plate readers. Public has little input because its a gift
summary: LVMPD's ~200-camera program (23,000+ searches) is funded via donations routed through a nonprofit, sidestepping Nevada's open-meeting laws.

headline: New Jersey Releases 2024 Audit of License Plate Reader Data Two Significant Violations Identified
summary: NJ's mandated ALPR audit of 523 agencies flagged two significant violations (users accessing data without required training); 2023 audit found two plus a complaint.

headline: 4 Investigates The push to regulate New Mexicos mass surveillance tools
summary: Sheriff John Allen told lawmakers his office caught a deputy using ALPR in the wrong way; deputy lost access and got a written reprimand.

headline: Police cameras tracked one driver 526 times in four months lawsuit says
summary: A suit alleges Westchester's ~600-reader network (1.6B scans) was shared with 50+ agencies incl. ICE; one plaintiff's car captured 2,400+ times.

headline: Federal immigration agents accessed Syracuse drivers data through secret Flock Safety deal
summary: Despite a contract barring outside sharing, SFPD... Syracuse PD inadvertently opted into the national network; data appeared in 175 CBP-related searches.

headline: A citys AI license plate cameras led an uproar a state emergency
summary: Troy expanded to 26 cameras without council approval; the mayor declared a public-safety 'state of emergency' to keep paying; council and auditor sued.

headline: Hillsborough town leaders terminate contract for license plate reader cameras
summary: Hillsborough canceled its 10-camera contract over data-sharing language that could let Flock disclose data to any government entity or third party.

headline: Dayton is covering Flock cameras with trash bags after officials found data use violated policy
summary: Dayton found 7,000+ immigration-related searches by outside entities, called them egregious violations, suspended the program, and covered all 72 cameras with trash bags.

headline: Area police share license plate reader data for immigration unstated reasons records show
summary: Greene and Montgomery County sheriffs logged 'Immigration (civil/administrative)' searches of Kettering data despite no ICE agreement; Kettering changed policy.

headline: How Cops Are Using Flock Safetys ALPR Network to Surveil Protesters and Activists
summary: Marion PD ran searches corresponding to the No Kings protest rallies in June and October 2025.

headline: Medford PD searched Eugenes Flock data. The reason? Hehehe
summary: An audit of Eugene's system showed 20 Medford searches logged 'Hehehe', alongside searches logged 'ICE', 'Immigration', and one referencing a woman who had an abortion.

headline: Oregon State Police violated law by sharing data with ICE for years lawsuit claims
summary: A lawsuit alleges OSP gave ICE access to LEDS/NLETS databases queried ~1.4M times in a year, violating Oregon's sanctuary law.

headline: Federal Immigration Officials Made 279 Queries into Bends Flock Safety Data in its First Three Weeks
summary: ICE/CBP/HSI queried Bend's data 279 times because a captain left the National Lookup default on; Bend disabled it and later shut the system off.

headline: Wyden slams surveillance tech company for ineffective protections for Oregonians
summary: Wyden found Flock's immigration/abortion search filters were easily bypassed and that ICE, CBP, Secret Service, and Navy had accessed the network.

headline: Former police officer who tracked estranged wifes car pleads guilty to stalking
summary: Officer Michael McSherry used plate readers to track his estranged wife and her mother 250+ times over nearly a decade; pleaded guilty to stalking.

headline: ACLU-PA statement on Philadelphia police surveillance van disguised as Google Streetview
summary: Philadelphia PD admitted disguising a license-plate-reader van with fake 'Google Streetview' decals to covertly scan plates.

headline: Flock cameras on campus raise privacy concerns
summary: Penn State installed 24 Flock ALPRs without public announcement and denied a student's Right-to-Know requests; cameras connect to the national network.

headline: Introduction to Flock cameras in Rhode Island
summary: ACLU-RI documented ~240-300 Flock ALPRs across RI, with most departments configured to share with other states and federal agencies incl. ICE.

headline: A license plate camera wrongly tagged car as stolen. Sisters sue Greenville police for arrest
summary: An ALPR wrongly flagged sisters' rental car as stolen; officers drew guns and handcuffed them despite the rental company having reported it returned.

headline: A company installed license plate cameras without permission. SC agency wants clear rules
summary: Flock installed 200+ cameras on SC state roads without authorization; SCDOT halted new approvals.

headline: SCPIF v SLED lawsuit challenging warrantless statewide ALPR database
summary: SLED runs a 100M+ scans/year database accessible to 70+ agencies created without legislative authorization; SCPIF sued to declare it unconstitutional.

headline: Shelby County deputy charged with stalking harassing ex-wife
summary: Deputy Thadius Gordon allegedly used a law-enforcement/ALPR database to track his ex-wife 100+ times; charged with stalking and relieved of duty.

headline: Flock Safety and Texas Sheriff Claimed License Plate Search Was for a Missing Person. It Was an Abortion Investigation
summary: Court records obtained by EFF showed the nationwide 83,000-camera search was a death investigation, contradicting the public missing-person claim.

headline: License Plate Surveillance Logs Reveal Racist Policing Against Romani People
summary: Grand Prairie PD searched an anti-Romani slur six times using Flock's Convoy feature.

city_agency: Houston PD / Texas DPS / Dallas PD
headline: Texas Police Are Running Constitutionally Questionable AI License Plate Searches for ICE
summary: At least 180 immigration-related Flock searches were logged by five Texas agencies since January 2025, tagged 'immigration', 'ICE+ERO', 'immigration status'.

headline: Texas DPS finds Flock surveillance violated state law by operating without a license
summary: Texas DPS issued Flock a cease-and-desist, finding it operated for years without the required private-security license for private installs.

headline: Austins license plate reader program will end this month
summary: Austin let its Flock contract lapse amid concern data could be shared with ICE; later DPS installed its own readers in Austin as a workaround.

headline: Utah Privacy Office Report 25-04 Automatic License Plate Readers
summary: State Privacy Office found both agencies lacked required retention schedules and one improperly authorized ALPR use beyond statutory limits.

city_agency: Vermont State Police / VT Intelligence Center
headline: Vermont State Auditor ALPR review (~11% of searches violated restrictions)
source: Office of the Vermont State Auditor
summary: A state-auditor review found ~11% of ALPR historical-data searches violated state restrictions on when officers may access the data.

headline: Virginia surveillance network tapped thousands of times for immigration cases
summary: VCIJ found ~3,000 immigration-related searches of Virginia's Flock network over a year, ~90% by out-of-state agencies, against departments' stated policies.

headline: Federal agency used Richmond license plate readers for immigration enforcement without permission
summary: The federal ATF searched Richmond PD's Flock data for immigration enforcement despite RPD having no intention of allowing such use.

headline: A federal judge ruled Norfolks Flock surveillance cameras dont invade peoples privacy yet
summary: Institute for Justice sued over 172+ cameras enabling warrantless surveillance; one plaintiff was logged 526 times in four months. Judge ruled it constitutional 'for now'; on appeal.

headline: Border Patrol ICE tapped into Washington police surveillance
summary: A UW Center for Human Rights report found Border Patrol searched at least 17 WA agencies' data via front/back/side doors, conflicting with the Keep Washington Working Act.

headline: Alien. Ice pick-up. immigration violation. (Spokane Flock immigration searches)
summary: Over 1,000 immigration-related searches hit Spokane County's network in early 2025 despite Washington's bar on using state resources for immigration enforcement.

headline: Protested this year? Cops mightve looked for you on Flock cams
summary: Records showed ~24 Spokane County Flock searches tied to protests and 50+ agencies nationally ran protest-related searches.

headline: Flock Safety setting allowed U.S. Border Patrol access to Wenatchee Valley license plate data
summary: A default Flock setting gave Border Patrol access to Wenatchee plate data without either department's knowledge; both disabled it.

headline: Former Milwaukee officer pleads guilty to misdemeanor for stalking ex-girlfriend with police technology
summary: Officer Josue Ayala searched Flock ~179 times tracking a woman he dated and her ex; pleaded guilty; MPD cut access from 370 to ~100 officers.

headline: Menasha police officer accused of using license plate recognition system to track his ex
summary: Officer Cristian Morales allegedly tried to use Flock at least five times to track his ex-girlfriend; resigned and bound over for trial on a felony misconduct count.

headline: Deputy On Leave Accused Of Misusing Police Databases To Track Coworker
summary: An internal investigation found Deputy Frank McGrath ran ~16 off-duty Flock searches with no case number to monitor a coworker he was involved with; resigned.

headline: Milwaukee PD accessed Illinois Flock cameras for classified investigation
summary: Audit data showed a Milwaukee PD intelligence unit ran searches against Illinois Flock cameras logged as a classified investigation.
